county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rock Island holds records from 1831. Rock Island is part of the Quad Cities metropolitan area (with Moline, East Moline, and Davenport, Iowa) — check Scott County, Iowa for related families. Rock Island Arsenal (established 1862) — military records. Black Hawk War connections. John Deere began manufacturing in Moline (1837) — employment records. Rock Island County Historical Society. Belgian immigrant community in Moline. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Swedish immigrants in Moline.

source_profiles.repository: Rock Island County Recorder, Rock Island
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1831. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Rock Island County Clerk, Rock Island
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1831. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.repository: Rock Island County Circuit Clerk, Rock Island
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1831. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Rock Island County Circuit Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Rock Island County Circuit Clerk, Rock Island
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1831. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Rock Island County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.repository: Rock Island County Circuit Clerk, Rock Island / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: African American Church and Community Records — Rock Island County
source_profiles.repository: Vivian G. Harsh Research Collection (Chicago Public Library) / local churches and historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: African American church records (AME, Baptist, COGIC), fraternal organization records, and community newspapers. The Great Migration (1910-1970) brought hundreds of thousands of African Americans from the South to Illinois, particularly Chicago and industrial cities. Chicago Defender newspaper is a critical source.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Vivian G. Harsh Collection at Chicago Public Library is the largest African American history collection in the Midwest

source_profiles.source_name: Swedish American Church Records — Rock Island County
source_profiles.repository: Swenson Swedish Immigration Research Center, Rock Island / Augustana Heritage Association
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Augustana Lutheran and other Swedish denomination church records — baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Bishop Hill Colony records (Henry County). Swenson Center at Augustana College holds the largest Swedish American research collection in the U.S.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Swenson Center has extensive microfilm and original records; many indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Harrisburg holds records from 1847. Saline County is named for the salt springs that were important in early Illinois history. Major coal mining county — mine employment records at Illinois State Archives document immigrant and Appalachian migrant workers. Check Gallatin County for pre-1847 records. Saline County Genealogical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Harrisburg tornado (1925, part of the Tri-State Tornado) caused significant damage. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records.

source_profiles.repository: Saline County Recorder, Harrisburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1847. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Saline County Clerk, Harrisburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1847. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.repository: Saline County Circuit Clerk, Harrisburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1847. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Saline County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / Illinois Department of Mines and Minerals
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspection reports, employment records, accident reports, and company records. Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. Annual coal reports published by the State of Illinois list mines and operators.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine inspection records at Illinois State Archives; some company records in local collections

source_profiles.source_name: Oil and Gas Lease Records — Saline County
source_profiles.repository: Saline County Recorder, Harrisburg
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease agreements recorded in county deed books. Illinois oil fields (particularly in southeastern Illinois) generated extensive lease and mineral rights records from the early 1900s onward. These records identify landowners and track property transactions.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved in county deed records

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Springfield holds records from 1821. Springfield is the state capital and home of the Illinois State Archives — the primary repository for Illinois genealogical research. Abraham Lincoln lived in Springfield from 1837 until he left for Washington in 1861 — Lincoln Home National Historic Site. Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum. Sangamon County Historical Society. Illinois State Genealogical Society is headquartered in Springfield. Created from Madison and Bond — check both for earlier records. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield.

source_profiles.repository: Sangamon County Recorder, Springfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Sangamon County Clerk, Springfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1821. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1821. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1821. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Sangamon County Clerk, Springfield / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1821. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Sangamon County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspection reports, employment records, accident reports, and company records. Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. Annual coal reports published by the State of Illinois list mines and operators.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine inspection records at Illinois State Archives; some company records in local collections

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.

source_profiles.repository: Vivian G. Harsh Research Collection (Chicago Public Library) / local churches and historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: African American church records (AME, Baptist, COGIC), fraternal organization records, and community newspapers. The Great Migration (1910-1970) brought hundreds of thousands of African Americans from the South to Illinois, particularly Chicago and industrial cities. Chicago Defender newspaper is a critical source.

source_profiles.source_name: Eighth Judicial Circuit Court Records — Sangamon County
source_profiles.repository: Sangamon County Circuit Clerk, Springfield / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Abraham Lincoln practiced law on Illinois' Eighth Judicial Circuit from 1837 to 1860. Circuit court records from this period include cases Lincoln argued and contemporaries he worked with. Court records also document the legal, economic, and social history of central Illinois families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Circuit court records at county level; Lincoln Legal Papers project has compiled many Lincoln-related documents

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rushville holds records from 1825. Schuyler County is in western Illinois along the Illinois River. Rushville was Scripps-Howard newspaperman Edward Scripps' birthplace. Check Pike and Fulton for pre-1825 records. Schuyler County Historical Society and Jail Museum. Military Tract land for War of 1812 veterans. Early settlers from New England, New York, and Upland South. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. Agricultural heritage along the Illinois River valley.

source_profiles.repository: Schuyler County Recorder, Rushville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1825. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1825. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1825. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Schuyler County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Military Tract Bounty Land Records — Schuyler County
source_profiles.repository: NARA / Illinois State Archives / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Military Tract between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers was reserved for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants. Many warrants were sold to speculators. Patent and warrant records identify original claimants and purchasers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-documented — patents at BLM-GLO; warrant records at NARA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Winchester holds records from 1839. Scott County is one of Illinois' smallest counties. Check Morgan County for pre-1839 records. Winchester was a modest county seat. Early settlers from Kentucky, Virginia, and Upland South. Scott County Historical Society. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield. Agricultural county — prairie farming records and township records supplement county records. Strong Methodist church records. Illinois River bluff communities. Lincoln practiced law on the circuit here.

source_profiles.repository: Scott County Recorder, Winchester
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1839. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Shelbyville holds records from 1827. Shelby County is in central Illinois — Lake Shelbyville (created 1970) area. Shelbyville was an important county seat on the road from Vandalia to Springfield. Check Fayette County for pre-1827 records. Shelby County Historical and Genealogical Society. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Early settlers from Kentucky and Tennessee — Upland South migration. Strong Methodist and Christian Church (Disciples) records. Findlay and Stewardson German communities.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Belleville holds records from 1790 — St. Clair is Illinois' oldest county, created under the Northwest Territory. French colonial heritage: Cahokia (1699) is one of the oldest European settlements in the Mississippi Valley — Cahokia parish registers are among the oldest in the Midwest. Belleville attracted massive German immigration from the 1830s — German-language records, Turner societies, and German Evangelical churches. Scott Air Force Base records. St. Clair County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Edwardsville. East St. Louis African American community.

source_profiles.repository: St. Clair County Recorder, Belleville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1790. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1790. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1790. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1790. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: St. Clair County Clerk, Belleville / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1790. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: French Colonial Catholic Parish Records — St. Clair County
source_profiles.repository: Diocese of Belleville / Illinois State Archives / Newberry Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: French Catholic parish registers from the Illinois Country — baptisms, marriages, and burials. Cahokia (1699), Kaskaskia (1703), and Prairie du Rocher parishes have records predating American settlement. Records in French and Latin. These are among the oldest church records in the Midwest.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish registers well-preserved; some transcribed and published; microfilm copies at multiple repositories

Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Toulon holds records from 1839. Stark County is one of Illinois' smallest counties. Created from Knox and Putnam — check both for pre-1839 records. Toulon is a small county seat. Wyoming (town) and Toulon are the primary communities. Stark County Historical Society. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Agricultural heritage. Early settlers from New England and New York. Strong Congregational and Methodist church records. Spoon River area.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Freeport holds records from 1837. Freeport was the site of a Lincoln-Douglas debate (1858) — the 'Freeport Doctrine.' Stephenson County attracted German and New England settlers. Freeport was an important railroad junction. Check Jo Daviess and Winnebago for pre-1837 records. Stephenson County Historical Society. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Silver Creek area German settlement. Cedarville was Jane Addams' childhood home. Strong Lutheran and Methodist church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.repository: Diocese of Belleville / Diocese of Springfield / local parishes
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Catholic and Lutheran parish records — baptisms, marriages, and burials. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. German settlement in Illinois was extensive, particularly in southwestern and central Illinois.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pekin holds records from 1827. Tazewell County is on the Illinois River. Pekin was once claimed to be the geographic center of the United States. Abraham Lincoln practiced law in Tazewell County — Pekin was on the Eighth Judicial Circuit. Check Sangamon County for pre-1827 records. Tazewell County Historical Society in Pekin. Mineral Springs area. IRAD at Illinois State University. German and Irish immigrant communities. East Peoria industrial heritage (Caterpillar). Strong agricultural records.

source_profiles.repository: Tazewell County Recorder, Pekin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1827. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Tazewell County Clerk, Pekin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1827. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.repository: Tazewell County Circuit Clerk, Pekin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1827. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Tazewell County Circuit Clerk, Pekin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1827. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Tazewell County Clerk, Pekin / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1827. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Tazewell County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.repository: Tazewell County Circuit Clerk, Pekin / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Eighth Judicial Circuit Court Records — Tazewell County
source_profiles.repository: Tazewell County Circuit Clerk, Pekin / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Abraham Lincoln practiced law on Illinois' Eighth Judicial Circuit from 1837 to 1860. Circuit court records from this period include cases Lincoln argued and contemporaries he worked with. Court records also document the legal, economic, and social history of central Illinois families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Circuit court records at county level; Lincoln Legal Papers project has compiled many Lincoln-related documents
